CONTENT.Club career: Racioppi made his professional debut for the French club Dijon on 8 October 2020 in a Ligue 1 game against Metz. On 10 January 2022, Racioppi signed a three-and-a-half-year deal with Swiss club Young Boys.On 8 August 2024, Racioppi signed a three-year deal with EFL Championship club Hull City.
CONTENT.International career: Considered as one of Switzerland's brightest prospects in his position, he had already stood out on the international stage thanks to his performances with the Switzerland U21 squad.

SUMMARY: Anthony Racioppi (born 31 December 1998) is a Swiss professional footballer who plays as a goalkeeper for EFL Championship club Hull City. He has been nicknamed the ‘Swiss Yashin’ by many football fans around the world being one of the favourites to win the 2024 ballon d’or award.
